{{Quote|This weapon can destroy a city… a landmass… or even an ''entire'' planet anywhere in the galaxy.|Emperor Palpatine|<ref name="DE2">''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]''</ref>}}
The '''Galaxy Gun''', or '''Galaxy Weapon''', was a [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] built by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]. It was commissioned by the "reborn" [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and designed by [[Umak Leth]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Ya see that? The Imps are building something big. What do ya think it is?|[[Shug Ninx]] to [[Salla Zend]] while on ''Salvager Three''|<ref name="DE2" />}}
[[File:Galaxy_Gun_egwt.png|thumb|left|180px|Galaxy Gun [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]]]]
The Galaxy Gun was a 7,250-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long [[Cannon/Legends|cannon]]-shaped [[Space station/Legends|space station]] built above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. The Galaxy Gun was almost five times the length of an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]]. Equipped with [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight thrusters]] and a slow Class Six [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], the Galaxy Gun was permanently stationed in Byss's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]].<ref name="NEGWT" />

The sole purpose of the Galaxy Gun was firing [[Missile/Legends|missiles]] outfitted with [[particle disintegrator warhead]]s,<ref name="EGWT" /> commonly referred to as "[[lightspeed torpedo]]es". These massive, high-yield explosives were launched from the Galaxy Gun before entering [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and emerging at precise coordinates virtually anywhere in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="NEGWT" /> Each missile was equipped with a fast hyperdrive,<ref name="EGWT" /> allowing it to travel at speeds through hyperspace that could rival the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']].<ref name="NEGWT" /> Once fired, a missile could travel from the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] in [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]].<ref name="EGWT" />

Dropping out of hyperspace, the projectile locked on to its targeted planet. As the missile flew towards its target, integrated defense systems were activated to defend the projectile. Automated [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] targeted incoming enemy vessels and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], while powerful [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] and [[Armor/Legends|armor]] plating protected the missile from [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] and [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] fire.<ref name="EGWT" />

On approach to the target, the particle disintegrator [[Warhead/Legends|warhead]] was energized by the [[Power core/Legends|power core]].<ref name="EGWT" /> Once the missile struck the planet, the energized warhead exploded, setting off a series of nucleonic chain reactions that would then spread across the surface, converting matter to energy that consumed everything in its path.<ref name="NEGWT" /> The missile had variable power settings; at full power, the projectile was capable of destroying an entire world if the nucleonic reactions were sustained until all available matter had been consumed. However, reduced power settings allowed the missile to be used as a tactical strike weapon, targeting and consuming a [[City/Legends|city]] or military base while leaving the rest of the planet unscathed.<ref name="EGWT" />

==Operational history==
{{Quote|They will return to Pinnacle Base and boast to their friends… They have at best a few hours to celebrate before they all die! It is time to show the Rebel Alliance its day is ended! Prepare to fire the Galaxy Weapon!|Emperor Palpatine|<ref name="DE2" />}}
[[File:Galaxygunoverbyss.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Imperial [[Warship/Legends|warships]] surrounding the Galaxy Gun]]
The Galaxy Gun was secretly constructed<ref name="NEGWT" /> in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]<ref name="ConstructionDate" /> in orbit over the planet Byss by order of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Palpatine intended for the Galaxy Gun to be a new weapon of terror through which he could destroy the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and reinstate his dominance over the galaxy. The Galaxy Gun was stationed at Byss, defended by the [[Byss Defense Fleet|massed Imperial fleet]]. Any attempt to destroy the superweapon would have forced the New Republic to engaged in a near-suicidal attack on the most heavily fortified world in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]].<ref name="EGWT" />

In retaliation for the [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|New Republic attack]] on his [[Emperor's Citadel|citadel]] and the escape of the surviving Rebels into hyperspace along with a group of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], Palpatine ordered the launch of the first projectile against the New Republic [[Pinnacle Base|base]] on [[Da Soocha V|Pinnacle Moon]].

Within hours, Pinnacle Moon had been [[Destruction of Da Soocha V|wiped]] off the face of the galaxy. However, due to the presence of spies in Byss itself, the New Republic forces along with some of the native [[Ixll]]s managed to escape the doomed moon in a fleet of transports.

[[File:ParticleDisintegratorWarhead-HBDE.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The Galaxy gun fires a particle disintegrator warhead at Pinnacle Moon]]

In response, New Republic commando and sabotage teams attacked the Galaxy Gun, but were annihilated by the massed Imperial fleet. The Emperor continued to launch projectiles, destroying entire unruly worlds and nearly crippling the New Republic. [[Krinemonen]] and [[Hirsi]] were devastated by the gun.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> As a result of this terrifying tactic, the Empire regained key territories in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner]] and Outer Rims in a relatively short time. Other known targets included [[Nespis VIII]]'s Space City, where even the New Republic's [[V-200 ion cannon|Kuat V-200 ion cannons]] were [[Destruction of the Nespis VIII spaceport|unable]] to penetrate the shielding of the projectiles, as well as the New Republic troopship ''[[Pelagia]]'' under the command of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Tekba]], which was carrying a hundred thousand troops at the time of its [[Destruction of the Pelagia|destruction]].

Ironically, Palpatine's excessive usage of the Galaxy Gun would end up being one of the major factors that led to his downfall: several Imperial officers, disturbed at their clone Emperor's erratic behavior, arranged for Royal Guard and member of [[Lumiya's Sith]] [[Carnor Jax]] to have the Emperor's genetic material tampered with down to the source material.

Despite the vast firepower possessed by the superweapon, the Galaxy Gun would be destroyed in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]] along with Byss and much of the massed Imperial fleet. With the Emperor's final death at the [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of]] [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], the famed [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] took control of the immense [[Eclipse-class dreadnought|''Eclipse''-variant super star destroyer]] ''[[Eclipse II]]''s computer banks and set the ship on a collision course with the Galaxy Gun. The Galaxy Gun was too clumsy and slow to evade the colliding Super Star Destroyer, which had devastating consequences.

The resulting explosion destroyed both superweapons. One of the victims killed in the destruction of the Galaxy Gun was [[Umak Leth]], the [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] who designed the superweapon. However, one last projectile exited the firing tube just before its destruction. The unguided missile was pulled in by Byss's gravitational field and drifted toward it. Then, the particle disintegrator warhead detonated and destroyed Byss, crushing the majority of the reborn Emperor's forces and loyal officers. The destruction of Byss and its inhabitants by the Galaxy Gun was a major factor for the ending of [[Operation Shadow Hand]].

==Legacy==
{{Quote|At the time, the Galaxy Gun was the most destructive weapon we'd ever seen. But I wish we had it now. We could blast those Yuuzhan Vong worldships right out of the sky.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="NEGWT" />}}
[[File:Galaxygunconstruction.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Galaxy Gun in construction over Byss]]

Nearly [[25 ABY/Legends|fifteen years]] later during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] would almost regret the destruction of the Galaxy Gun, as it could have helped the New Republic wipe out thousands of [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Koros-Strohna]] worldships.<ref name="NEGWT" /> Over a century after Palpatine's death and the Yuuzhan Vong invasion, [[Vul Isen]] of the [[One Sith]] created viral spores that could poison planets. After being confronted by [[Cade Skywalker]], Isen boasted over the Galactic Empire no longer needing the Galaxy Gun or the Death Star to destroy planets compared to his science.

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Deathstardesigner.jpg|thumb|Galaxy Gun (lower left) as it appears in ''[[Death Star Designer]]'']]
In the online game ''[[Death Star Designer]]'', Galaxy Guns can be added to a player's Death Star model. It could be developed for 300 billion credits.
